data.title: Pacific Basin Shipping reports 1H results

data.title: MetLife signals continued growth in Asia and Latin America with 9% sales increase, outlines strategic moves amid dynamic market environment
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: MetLife, Inc. (MET) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Michel Abbas Khalaf opened by stating, "During the second quarter, we continued to navigate an evolving and dynamic economic environment, while executing against our New Frontier growth strategy. We demonstrated all weather performance and clear momentum across business segments posting strong sales in many markets, executing strategic transactions maintaining a laser focus on managing expenses and returning capital to shareholders." Khalaf also emphasized, "Although the second quarter does not demonstrate the full earnings power of MetLife, we're confident in our ability to deliver on the commitments of our New Frontier strategy."
  • Khalaf highlighted adjusted earnings of $1.4 billion, or $2.02 per share, and noted, "This reflects less favorable underwriting, albeit within normal fluctuations and less favorable investment margins versus a year ago." He cited a quarterly adjusted return on equity of 14.6% and a direct expense ratio of 11.7%, beating the annual target.
  • Khalaf announced a strategic partnership with Workday to expand the reach of MetLife's benefits experience platform, and highlighted new strategic transactions, including the acquisition of PineBridge Investments, the formation of Chariot Re with an initial $10 billion reinsurance deal, and a risk transfer deal with Talcott Financial Group.
  • CFO John Dennis McCallion stated, "Adjusted earnings were $1.4 billion, down 16% and down 15% on a constant currency basis. The primary drivers were less favorable underwriting and lower investment margins. These were partially offset by volume growth and favorable expense margins year-over- year."

Outlook

  • Management expects improvements in the nonmedical health interest-adjusted benefit ratio, with John Dennis McCallion stating, "For the nonmedical health interest adjusted benefit ratio, we expect the Q3 ratio show an approximate 200 basis point improvement from Q2 levels."
  • Khalaf said, "We are encouraged by the underlying momentum across our businesses as we head into the back half of the year."
  • EMEA's quarterly run rate is expected to remain above its 2025 quarterly guidance of $70 million to $75 million for the remainder of the year.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted earnings per share were $2.02, with adjusted earnings of $1.4 billion.
  • Group Benefits adjusted earnings were $400 million, with the Group Life mortality ratio at 83%, below the 2025 target range but higher than the prior year's 79.1%.
  • The non-medical health interest-adjusted benefit ratio was 74.8%, modestly above the annual target range of 69% to 74%.
  • Group Benefit sales are up 9% year-to-date; adjusted premiums, fees and other revenues grew 4% from a year ago.
  • Retirement and Income Solutions (RIS) adjusted earnings were $368 million; total liability exposures were up 6% from a year ago.
  • Asia adjusted earnings were $350 million; sales rose 9% on a constant currency basis, with 29% and 36% sales jumps in Japan and Korea, respectively.
  • Latin America adjusted earnings totaled $233 million, matching its all-time quarterly high.
  • EMEA posted near-record adjusted earnings of $100 million, up 30% on both a reported and constant currency basis.
  • MetLife Holdings adjusted earnings were $144 million, down 6%.
  • Cash and liquid assets at holding companies were $5.2 billion; $900 million was returned to shareholders via dividends and buybacks in Q2.

Q&A

  • Suneet Kamath, Jefferies: Asked about elevated claims and Chariot Re's outlook. Ramy Tadros responded, "We don't see any macro impacts here... What's different about this quarter is normally our results benefits from diversification across these products... What is unusual here is that these products all moved in one direction for us."
  • Ryan Krueger, KBW: Asked about sales strength in Japan and Korea. Lyndon Oliver said, "Asia's year-to-date sales through the first half have increased 10% when you look year-over-year... In Korea, the Korea sales have grown 41% when you look at the first half year-over-year sales."
  • Thomas Gallagher, Evercore: Asked about disability pressure and CECL reserves. Tadros clarified, "We are really talking here about 2 clients... related to injuries, which have very high claim amounts... nothing to do with any of the other macro factors."
  • Elyse Greenspan, Wells Fargo: Inquired about long-term care and Holdings transactions. Tadros replied, "We continue to be looking at them and an active dialogue in terms of price discovery as well as structured discovery, but these are complex."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on clarification of underlying trends and expressing interest in Asia and LatAm growth drivers. Questions reflected a desire for more detail on outlook and normalization of ratios.
  • Management maintained a confident and slightly defensive tone, especially when addressing claims volatility. Khalaf reiterated, "We are confident in our ability to deliver on the commitments of our New Frontier strategy." Tadros emphasized normalization and lack of macro issues, while McCallion provided specifics on expected improvements.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's sentiment remains confident but with more detailed explanations for short-term fluctuations. Analysts' tone shifted from cautious optimism to a more probing stance on segment-specific risks and normalization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted earnings per share increased from $1.96 in Q1 to $2.02 in Q2, while adjusted earnings rose from $1.3 billion to $1.4 billion.
  • The direct expense ratio improved from 12% in Q1 to 11.7% in Q2.
  • Group Benefits sales growth accelerated from 2% in Q1 to 9% year-to-date in Q2.
  • Asia sales momentum improved, with Japan and Korea showing substantial sales increases, while Asia earnings declined on lower margins.
  • Strategic activity intensified, with the Chariot Re launch and PineBridge acquisition highlighted as drivers for future growth.
  • Analysts in Q2 focused more on claims volatility and normalization, compared to broader macro and capital management topics in Q1.
  • Management's confidence in the New Frontier strategy remains consistent, but with greater emphasis on normalization of recent volatility and execution of strategic transactions.

Risks and Concerns

  • Less favorable underwriting and investment margins were highlighted as key challenges.
  • Group Life and non-medical health products experienced elevated but non-trending claims.
  • CECL reserves increased for commercial mortgage loans, with McCallion stating, "We did see an uptick in reserves, a little over $200 million in commercial mortgages and we've anticipated some charge-offs and some losses would emerge in 2025."
  • Complexity and timing risks were noted in potential long-term care block transactions.
  • No macro factors were identified as driving current claims volatility, and management expects normalization in subsequent quarters.

Final Takeaway

MetLife's second quarter results reflected strong sales growth in Asia and Latin America, disciplined expense management, and significant progress on strategic initiatives such as Chariot Re and the PineBridge acquisition. While certain segments experienced elevated claims and lower investment margins, management emphasized these were within normal ranges and expects improvements in the second half of the year. The company's focus remains on responsible growth, attractive returns, and leveraging its diversified global platform in line with its New Frontier strategy.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Lenzing Aktiengesellschaft reports 1H results
data.description:
• Lenzing Aktiengesellschaft press release (OTCPK:LNZNF): 1H Revenue grows to EUR 1.34 bn, EBITDA at EUR 268.6 mn.

  • Earnings after tax positive at EUR 15.2 mn.

  • EBITDA grew by 63.3 percent to EUR 268.6 mn.

  • The EBITDA margin rose from 12.5 percent to 20 percent. Earnings before interest and tax (EBIT) amounted to EUR 109 mn (compared with EUR 18.9 mn in the same period of the previous year)

  • Lenzing Group confirms its guidance for the 2025 financial year of year-on-year higher EBITDA.

data.title: Shoals Technologies raised at Roth, seeing investor opportunity after post-earnings drop
data.description:
Shoals Technologies (NASDAQ:SHLS) +7% in Thursday's trading as Roth Capital upgraded shares to Buy from Hold with a $10 price target, raised from $5, after the solar company delivered a Q2 beat and raise that showed strong bookings and backlog.

Roth's Philip Shen said the stock market's surprisingly negative reaction to the strong print - apparently based on concerns that revenue growth is coming at the expense of margins - as a buying opportunity given the company's premium product, greater than 50% market share, and strong growth trajectory backed by a visible, high quality backlog.

While Shoals (NASDAQ:SHLS) management is growing revenue in part with lower margin offerings, the analyst said he expects the company will ultimately return to its long-term target.

Shen said Shoals (SHLS) has undertaken a strategic initiative during recent quarters to boost market share by appealing to new customers the company historically did not target, gain wallet share with existing customers, and attract prior customers that may have stopped using its products, and the lower gross margins have come from the use of promotional pricing during this time as well as from product mix where some customers are using lower margin products such as combiner boxes or the "home run" solution.

Shen said he sees the initiative as an investment by trading off short-term margin to gain higher margin later, when Shoals (SHLS) can sell its higher margin solutions to the new customers; while it is possible the market could at some point force sales at lower prices, he expects the company to maintain discipline and not continuing to sell at promotional pricin and lower margins in perpetuity.

data.title: Brilliant Earth raises 2025 net sales growth guidance to 2.5%-4% following strong Q2 performance
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Brilliant Earth Group, Inc. (BRLT) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Beth Tanara Gerstein announced "a strong quarter where we drove positive year-over-year net sales growth and far exceeded the high end of our guidance for both net sales and profitability." She stated that the company is "raising our annual net sales guidance" and highlighted acceleration in Q3 to date.
  • Gerstein emphasized a "onetime dividend and distribution of approximately $25.3 million in the aggregate," reflecting a commitment to rewarding shareholders, a strong balance sheet, and confidence in the ability to generate cash while funding future growth.
  • The CEO pointed to "best year-over-year top line growth in the past 18 months," with net sales growth of 3.3%, adjusted EBITDA of $3.2 million, and net cash up 5% year-over-year to $99 million at the end of Q2.
  • Product momentum was underscored by a 38% year-over-year bookings increase in fine jewelry and notable celebrity partnerships, including custom designs for Beyonce and a new sports partnership with tennis star Madison Keys.
  • Gerstein cited showroom expansion, technology and AI investments, and a strong omnichannel approach as continued growth drivers.
  • CFO Chuenhong Kuo stated, "Q2 net sales were $108.9 million, up 3.3% year-over-year, exceeding the top end of our guidance range by 330 basis points. Total orders grew 18% year-over-year and repeat orders grew 11% year-over-year in the second quarter."
  • Kuo highlighted, "Q2 gross margin was 58.3%, within our medium-term gross margin target in the high 50s and a 250 basis point decline over Q2 last year... Q2 adjusted EBITDA of $3.2 million, or a 2.9% adjusted EBITDA margin, far exceeding our guidance range. This marks our 16th consecutive quarter of profitability."
  • The CFO also discussed strategic inventory procurement in response to tariffs and announced the full repayment of the term loan, leaving "no outstanding debt on our balance sheet."

Outlook

  • The company expects Q3 net sales to grow 8% to 10% year-over-year and adjusted EBITDA between $3 million and $4.5 million.
  • For the full year, net sales growth guidance has been raised to 2.5% to 4% year-over-year, driven by engagement ring performance, showroom growth, fine jewelry strength, and favorable comps.
  • Adjusted EBITDA margin guidance remains at 3% to 4% for 2025.
  • Management warned of potential downward gross margin impact from gold and platinum spot prices and new tariffs, notably a 25% tariff on all imports from India effective August 27, while expressing confidence in mitigating these effects through sourcing flexibility and pricing optimization.

Financial Results

  • Q2 net sales reached $108.9 million, representing a 3.3% year-over-year increase.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was $3.2 million, with a 2.9% margin, and net cash stood at $99 million, up $5 million year-over-year.
  • Operating expense was 59.4% of net sales, and marketing expense was 24.1% of net sales, with a 180 basis point year-over-year leverage.
  • Inventory grew 24% year-over-year due to procurement ahead of tariffs, while inventory turns remained significantly higher than the industry average.
  • The company repurchased $200,000 of common stock in Q2, bringing total repurchases to $1 million.
  • A onetime cash dividend and distribution of $0.25 per share (totaling approximately $25.3 million) was announced, with payment set for September 8, 2025.

Q&A

  • Christopher Brazeau, KeyBanc: Asked about fine jewelry margin dynamics and purchase habits. Gerstein responded that "consumers continue to be very discerning in terms of fine jewelry... we're just continuing to see increased traction," with no significant change in pricing or AOV trends compared to previous months.
  • Brazeau asked about capital deployment following debt repayment. Gerstein indicated ongoing investment in the showroom expansion, digital leadership, and brand growth, supported by "a very strong cash balance sheet." Kuo added both dividend and debt payoff "stem from our strong balance sheet and cash position... and there will be net interest savings."
  • Unidentified Analyst (for Oliver Chen, TD Securities): Inquired about consumer health, price point strength, and showroom uplift. Gerstein said the consumer is "discerning" and that the company is "outperforming the market," noting high single-digit growth in engagement rings and bands. She also highlighted "80-plus percent growth with those retail type of consumers" in showroom walk-in traffic.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts asked pointed questions about margin sustainability, capital allocation, and consumer spending, but maintained a neutral-to-slightly-positive tone, congratulating management on results and referencing strong consumer traction.
  • Management's tone was confident during prepared remarks and the Q&A, emphasizing resilience and strategic execution. Gerstein described fine jewelry traction as "something that we're strategically investing in" and repeatedly asserted the company's market outperformance.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, current management sentiment is more positive, with greater emphasis on acceleration and raised guidance, while analysts remain constructively inquisitive rather than skeptical.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance shifted upward from 1%-3% annual net sales growth to 2.5%-4% following outperformance in Q2, with Q3 sales growth also expected to accelerate.
  • Showroom expansion and fine jewelry continued as strategic focal points, but the Q2 call added a stronger emphasis on celebrity partnerships and cultural relevance.
  • Gross margin declined by 250 basis points year-over-year (compared to 130 basis points in Q1), mainly due to gold costs and tariffs, but remained within the target range.
  • Net sales reversed from a 3.5% year-over-year decline in Q1 to 3.3% growth in Q2, and adjusted EBITDA nearly tripled from $1.1 million in Q1 to $3.2 million in Q2.
  • Analysts in both quarters focused on engagement trends, fine jewelry growth, margin outlook, and capital allocation, though Q2 questions were more forward-looking given the company's strengthened position and dividend announcement.
  • Management tone showed increased confidence, supported by exceeding guidance and the launch of a shareholder dividend.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited the risk of new tariffs, especially the 25% tariff on all imports from India effective August 27, and the impact of gold and platinum price volatility.
  • Kuo stated, "We have not yet fully determined the financial impact of this development on our business, and we are actively analyzing how this informs our operating plan."
  • Management outlined mitigation strategies including geographic supply chain diversity, rapid sourcing adjustments, dynamic pricing, and procurement optimization.
  • Macro environment and consumer spending trends remain areas of close monitoring.

Final Takeaway

Brilliant Earth delivered strong Q2 results with accelerated sales growth and profitability, prompting a raised full-year net sales outlook. The company emphasized strategic progress in fine jewelry, showroom expansion, and brand partnerships, while announcing a substantial onetime dividend and the full repayment of debt. Management expressed confidence in navigating tariff and commodity headwinds through operational flexibility and data-driven strategies, supporting continued profitable growth and shareholder value creation for the remainder of 2025.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SUSS MicroTec SE reports 1H results
data.description:
• SUSS MicroTec SE press release (OTCPK:SESMF): 1H Sales rose significantly in the first few months, reaching an outstanding €266.4 million (previous year: €192.8 million).
  • EBIT rose significantly from €30.1 million to €41.9 million.
  • EBIT margin in the first half of the year was 15.7%, slightly above the previous year's figure of 15.6%.
  • Outlook:Management Board confirms the sales target of €470 to €510 million for the financial year 2025. The gross profit margin is expected to range from 37 to 39%. The EBIT margin is expected to be 13 to 15% for the year.

data.title: Sen. Warren calls on Fed's Cook to activate capital buffer tool for banks
data.description:
U.S. Senator Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) pressed Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook to deploy the so-called countercyclical capital buffer tool, warning that President Donald Trump's erratic tariff policies might hinder the banking system and broader economy, according to a media report dated Thursday.

Another area of concern, Warren wrote in a letter to Cook, is growing vulnerabilities in private credit markets since the great recession over a decade ago, Bloomberg reported.

The capital buffer in question was authorized by Congress for the Fed to require banks to hold extra capital during economic expansions to bolster their resilience against potential future downturns.

Although banks have consistently claimed that stricter regulations boost lending costs and disadvantage them relative to global rivals, Warren dismissed the notion that easing rules would hurt lending and harm banks.

“Better capitalized banks lend more to businesses and households and are better positioned to buoy the economy in a recession – a reality growing increasingly likely in light of President Trump’s economic mismanagement,” Warren said in the letter, as quoted by Bloomberg. “The Fed is inexplicably moving in the opposite direction, weakening big bank safeguards at the worst possible moment.”

The Fed considers a number of factors in assessing financial stability and whether to activate the countercyclical capital buffer, including asset-liability mismatch, leverage and asset values.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: NN, Inc. reiterates $430M–$460M sales guidance while targeting 13%–14% EBITDA margin amid portfolio shift
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: NN, Inc. (NNBR) Q2 2025

Management View

  • Harold C. Bevis, CEO, opened the call stating, “We had a pretty solid quarter. Our sales were right at $107.9 million. That's adjusted for the sale of Lubbock last year. Our adjusted EBITDA came in at $13.2 million, which was 12.2% of sales. Our operating income -- adjusted operating income came in at $4.9 million and our adjusted net income was $0.02 per diluted share.”
  • Bevis highlighted nearly 20% adjusted gross margins, “We got really close to 20%, 19.5% adjusted gross margins.” He also emphasized new business momentum: “We gained a lot of new business for future periods, $32.7 million year-to-date, which put us on pace for our annual goal this year of $65 million.”
  • Bevis reported a sales portfolio shift: “39% of our sales was in automotive and 61% non-automotive. So that's a strategic goal of ours is to balance our portfolio.”
  • Strategic refinancing was noted: “We refinanced our term loan and we're now focused fully on reducing the cost of our term loan as well as refinancing our preferred stock.”
  • Market dynamics were addressed, including global automotive turbulence, the resurgence of internal combustion engine (ICE) programs, and growing commercial vehicle and medical equipment segments.
  • COO Timothy M. French described operational transformation, stating, “Since June 2023, we reduced our staffing by more than 600 or approximately 20%. However, this is not exclusively about reduction. Although we reduced by more than 700, we also strategically added approximately 80 people in those areas where we are seeing the most growth and to also increase our talent base in areas of the company such as sales and engineering.”
  • French outlined margin progress: “Year-to-date, our 18.2% adjusted gross margin is an expansion of 190 basis points. We'll continue to focus on further margin expansion. As part of our longer term plan, we have an internal goal to achieve approximately 20% gross margin, which based on our current results, we believe is achievable.”
  • CFO Christopher H. Bohnert stated, “On an as-reported basis, net sales for the quarter were $107.9 million, declining about $15.1 million versus last year's second quarter. On a pro forma basis, accounting for the adjustments I noted earlier, net sales modestly declined 2.4% or $2.7 million.”
  • Bohnert added, “Our adjusted operating income for the second quarter was $4.9 million, marking a strong increase of $2.8 million compared to the $2.1 million in the prior year second quarter.”
  • Management announced the hiring of a new Chief Commercial Officer, Tim Erro, and a new CTO, enhancing focus on electrical and medical segments. Bevis revealed, “we now have a core team who understands electrical cable assemblies and we're evaluating an organic entry into that market just as we've done for medical products.”

Outlook

  • Bohnert reiterated guidance: “Net sales, we're still expecting in the range of $430 million to $460 million, adjusted EBITDA of $53 million to $63 million. However, we are leaning toward the lower half of the range on both those guidances.”
  • Free cash flow guidance remained at $14 million to $16 million. “New business wins, again, no change, $60 million to $70 million.”
  • Management cautioned that guidance “reflects the uncertainty from some of our top customers that we're hearing in the marketplace as well as the unstable macroeconomic environment, but we are holding our guidance at this point.”

Financial Results

  • Net sales for Q2 were $107.9 million. Adjusted EBITDA was $13.2 million. Adjusted operating income stood at $4.9 million, and adjusted net income was $0.02 per diluted share.
  • Adjusted gross margin reached 19.5%. Adjusted EBITDA margin was 12.2% of net sales, expanding by 130 basis points as reported.
  • Power Solutions segment net sales were $44.6 million, with adjusted EBITDA at $9.1 million and margin of 20.4%. Mobile Solutions segment net sales were $63.4 million, adjusted EBITDA $8.6 million, with margin climbing to 13.6%.
  • Year-to-date, 70 new programs launched; management expects 112 new programs in 2025, contributing $26 million to this year’s top line and $48 million at peak run rate. The new business pipeline reached $750 million.
  • Capital expenditures are slated at $18 million to $20 million for the year, with a focus on growth in medical and China auto markets.

Q&A

  • Robert Duncan Brown, Lake Street Capital: Asked about incremental margin on new business wins and ability to take share. Bevis explained, “for new business wins… if we have existing open capacity and our costs are fully covered in the plants. And we price that to win. We'll go down to 15% on that type of a business…for any new investments, we have our floor at 25% ROI for the investments…it's accretive by 3 or 4 points on the EBITDA line overall.”
  • Brown inquired about electrical and data center markets. Bevis shared, “on the power generation side, where we directly participate in that, we have been growing. So overall, it's a growth area for our company.”
  • John Edward Franzreb, Sidoti & Company: Probed for drivers to reach low-end guidance. Bevis clarified, “We're going to be benefiting though from our new business launches…we're counting on those programs not getting pushed out.”
  • Franzreb asked about capital expenditures. French responded, “The bulk of it is in new business programs… the vast majority of it is to support the growth.”
  • Michael Roy Crawford, B. Riley Securities: Requested specifics on on-time delivery improvements. French said, “we now have green scorecards with all of our customers, meaning that we are a supplier in good standing, and in some cases, identified as a preferred supplier.”
  • Crawford sought capacity details for dedicated medical machines. Bevis explained, “The business right now is $15 million to $18 million run rate. So 2 shift is normal for us. So right away, just in terms of normal, we have double the amount of sales possibility there, $30 million to $35 million.”
  • Hans Baldau, NOBLE Capital: Asked about M&A program details. Bevis noted, “We are being very -- I'm leading it myself personally. We're being very specific with the type of acquisitions we're looking at. We have several active processes underway.”

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on guidance achievability, capital allocation, and growth areas. Questions pointed to uncertainties in market timing and execution risk, especially around new program launches and M&A.
  • Management’s tone remained confident and measured during prepared remarks, with Bevis frequently emphasizing progress and transformation. In Q&A, management displayed transparency, detailed responses, and readiness to address challenges, but also acknowledged risks: “It's possible that if people get nervous, they could push out launch dates on us and we've had a little bit of that this year.”
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a confident yet realistic tone, reiterating guidance but increasingly noting external uncertainties. Analysts continued to probe execution risk and the pace of transformation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for net sales and adjusted EBITDA was reiterated, but management signaled a tilt toward the lower end, citing customer and macroeconomic uncertainties—a nuanced shift from the prior quarter’s firmer outlook.
  • Strategic focus remains on portfolio diversification, operational efficiency, and new business wins, with incremental progress in staffing, new leadership, and capital deployment for targeted growth areas.
  • Analysts sustained their focus on margin improvement, capital allocation, and the execution pace of new business launches, consistent with previous quarter’s questions.
  • Key metrics such as gross margin and EBITDA margin further improved, while the number of new program launches was slightly reduced due to timing adjustments in the automotive sector.
  • Management confidence in transformation efforts remains high, with an uptick in detail about M&A strategy and organic market entries.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified uncertainties in the global automotive market, including “cloudiness or uncertainty with the tariffs, vehicle affordability, high interest rates, fading electric vehicle incentives and the emergence of China as the global exporter of choice.”
  • The risk of delayed new program launches was cited as a key factor that could impact revenue guidance.
  • Sticky working capital balances, driven by rising metal prices, continue to challenge cash flow, though management outlined plans to reduce working capital as a percentage of sales.
  • Analysts raised concerns about execution risk around capital expenditures, new market entries, and the pace of M&A integration.

Final Takeaway

NN, Inc. management emphasized steady progress in its transformation strategy, with further margin improvements, a balanced sales portfolio, and ongoing cost discipline. The company remains confident in achieving its full-year targets while acknowledging external headwinds and execution risks, particularly in automotive and new business launches. Expansion into medical and electrical markets, alongside a revitalized M&A strategy and leadership additions, positions NN for continued growth, supported by robust new business wins and a growing pipeline.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
